chunk_summary: This section outlines the workflow for processing donor milk in milk banks, from intake to release, emphasizing quality assurance, safety protocols, and adherence to national guidelines for lactation management centers.
learning_objectives: Understand the step-by-step workflow for processing donor milk in milk banks.
Learn the quality assurance standards and safety protocols for human milk banking.
Explore national guidelines and best practices for lactation management centers.
Identify key considerations for donor milk intake and release processes.
key_facts: Donor milk undergoes rigorous screening, pasteurization, and microbiological testing before release.
National guidelines recommend adherence to the CLMC module for quality assurance in milk banking.
Human milk banks aim to provide safe donor milk to vulnerable infants, especially preterm or low-birth-weight babies.
The eCLMC implementation guide highlights the importance of tracking donor milk from intake to release.
Microbiological testing ensures donor milk meets safety standards before distribution.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: National guidelines emphasize the importance of microbiological testing and pasteurization to ensure the safety of donor milk before release. (Source: https://nhm.gov.in/images/pdf/programmes/IYCF/National_Guidelines_Lactation_Management_Centres.pdf)
document_key_topics: Quality assurance in human milk banking
